search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae conjunctivitis allergic telehealth guideline PDF
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Medicine selection and doses were outside documentation scope.
The workflow must not infer allergic or infectious cause.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for guideline-described allergic conjunctivitis features and red-eye warnings; other allergic eye disease, keratitis, uveitis, glaucoma, trauma, and paediatric qualifiers are not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Direct for DHA telehealth history and red-flag review; physical eye examination, diagnosis, medicine, referral, and urgency remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: Verified for Dubai Health Authority telehealth services.
recency_verification: The current DHA Issue 2 conjunctivitis PDF, due for revision 2029-02-21, was reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer official DHA conjunctivitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports allergic-conjunctivitis terminology, itch, watering, redness, seasonal or bilateral pattern, and assessed conjunctival, eyelid, or discharge fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Describes unilateral or bilateral redness, watery, mucus or purulent discharge, crusting, itching, burning, grittiness, foreign-body sensation, contact spread, dry-eye symptoms, and mechanical or chemical insult context.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports specific vision, severe-pain, photophobia, trauma, corneal, and pupil warning prompts only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists reduced visual acuity, ciliary flush, photophobia, severe foreign-body sensation, corneal opacity, fixed pupil, severe headache with nausea, post-traumatic red eye, and immune compromise.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports contact-lens and allergy history plus clinician-assessed visual-acuity and ocular findings.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires history and visual review for red eye and discharge, distinguishes discharge character, asks about vision, foreign-body sensation, photophobia, trauma and contact lenses, and highlights keratitis risk in contact-lens wearers.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-documented allergen avoidance, eye hygiene, supportive care, return, worsening, and follow-up without medicine or dose generation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-led management, contact-lens hygiene or cessation discussion, response review, infection-control education, and referral for persistent symptoms, reduced acuity, photophobia, corneal opacity, painful red eye, severe headache with nausea, hyphema, trauma, or inability to open the eye; dose tables are not mapped.

unresolved_source_gaps: Fixed duration presets, fluorescein testing, a fixed one-to-two-week interval, and the combined history draft remain unsupported.
Allergic conjunctivitis remains clinician-stated; the workflow must not recommend antihistamines, steroids, antibiotics, eye drops, or referral automatically.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
